securityPage.metaDescription: How Sovereign keeps private work on customer-owned hardware, and best-practice patterns for connecting Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace under IT control.
securityPage.eyebrow: Security brief · For IT & security reviewers
securityPage.title: Your perimeter. Your Sovereign. Not our cloud.
securityPage.lead: Public AI tools pull company mail and documents into someone else’s platform. A Sovereign does the opposite: computation stays under the owner’s roof. Centurion Limited does not host, proxy, or train on your private world.
securityPage.thesisTitle: The objection we hear from IT
securityPage.thesisBody: Teams often assume “AI connected to Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace” means unsafe exfiltration through a vendor cloud. With Sovereign, mail and documents are accessed only under your identity provider’s rules, with tokens kept on the owner’s machine. Centurion Limited never sits in the middle of that traffic.
securityPage.benefitOwnBody: The Sovereign lives on customer-owned hardware. Private chat, documents, and mission work stay with the owner — not in a multi-tenant AI product.
securityPage.benefitCryptoBody: The iPhone companion talks to the Sovereign through Crypto Chat: end-to-end encrypted on the owner’s network. Public chatbots have no equivalent.
securityPage.benefitOfflineBody: When nothing may leave the room, Sovereign can operate without the public internet. Connectivity is a choice, not a dependency.
securityPage.benefitSkillsBody: Sovereign does not install skills from unknown vendors. It builds what the owner needs locally, so third-party skills cannot quietly leak mail or documents.
securityPage.benefitHumanBody: Serious actions stay under human authority. The machine works at machine speed; the owner decides when something leaves their control.
securityPage.benefitNoTrainTitle: We do not train on your content
securityPage.benefitNoTrainBody: Centurion Limited does not receive private Sovereign inference data to train shared models. Your mail and documents are not our product fuel.
securityPage.boundaryCaption: Figure · Private world stays home; company and public chat stay outside
securityPage.diagramCompareCaption: Figure · Public AI pulls mail into a vendor cloud; Sovereign keeps work under your roof
securityPage.diagramMailCaption: Figure · Your IdP grants OAuth to the Sovereign; Centurion Limited never relays mail
securityPage.emailLead: When a principal needs mail assistance, IT should approve the same patterns used for any trusted desktop client: OAuth under your tenant, least privilege, revocable consent, and no third-party mail relay.
securityPage.m365Intro: Prefer Microsoft Graph with Entra ID (Azure AD) app registration — not shared passwords or unmanaged IMAP.
securityPage.m365Items: Register an application in Entra ID under your tenant|Choose delegated access for a named user, or application permissions only if IT policy requires it|Request least-privilege Graph scopes for mail (read and/or send only as needed)|Require admin consent for organisational policy|Store refresh tokens only on the Sovereign — never in Centurion Limited systems|Revoke or rotate access from Entra at any time|Do not route mail through a Centurion cloud gateway
securityPage.googleIntro: Prefer Google OAuth with a Workspace-admin–controlled client — not password-based access.
securityPage.googleItems: Create an OAuth client in Google Cloud under your organisation|Allowlist the client in Google Workspace Admin as required|Request least-privilege Gmail scopes only|Store tokens only on the Sovereign|Revoke access from Workspace Admin or the user’s Google account|Do not route mail through a Centurion cloud gateway
securityPage.sharedItems: Mail content is processed on the Sovereign under the owner’s roof|Centurion Limited does not proxy, store, or train on company mail|IT retains control through the identity provider|If policy requires air-gap, leave mail disconnected — Sovereign still works offline|Document the approved scopes and consent path in your change record
securityPage.honestBody: We do not claim FedRAMP, SOC 2, IL ratings, or military authorisations. Our position is architectural: owner-controlled hardware, encrypted private chat on your network, optional offline operation, and IdP-governed connectors. For a private security questionnaire or architecture review, contact us.
securityPage.rolloutLead: A path business owners can propose to IT when policy prefers a narrow start.
securityPage.rollout1Body: Dedicated host. Manual approvals. No YOLO. No messaging gateway.
securityPage.rollout2Body: Pair that owner’s iPhone only. Review logs and provider traffic.
securityPage.rollout3Body: Init and unlock sealed long-term memory. Still owner-only.
securityPage.rollout4Body: Allowlists, IdP mail review, then more users. Docker isolation if required.
securityPage.faqLead: Clear answers to the objections we hear most often — data, autonomy, access, and liability.
securityPage.faq1Q: Does connecting AI to Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace send our mail to your cloud?
securityPage.faq1A: No. Mail is accessed from the Sovereign under your identity provider’s rules. Tokens stay on the owner’s machine. Centurion Limited does not host, proxy, or sit in the middle of company mail traffic.
securityPage.faq2Q: Where do conversations and API keys live?
securityPage.faq2A: On the Sovereign brain — hardware you own, typically under the Centurion home directory on that machine. Crypto Chat history stays on the brain. Centurion Limited does not host your private chat history. Memory Horizon (Hot Cache, Session Recall, optional Vault) is explained at https://help.1human1ai.com/docs/privacy-and-security/memory-horizon-and-vault
securityPage.faq3Q: Can the agent run destructive commands without a human?
securityPage.faq3A: Not by default. Dangerous commands require human approval (manual mode). Timeouts fail closed (deny). A hardline blocklist cannot be turned off by “allow always” shortcuts. For stronger isolation, run tools inside Docker or another sandbox backend.
securityPage.faq4Q: Who can talk to our Sovereign?
securityPage.faq4A: Access is default-deny. Messaging gateways use allowlists or pairing; an empty allowlist admits nobody. Remote (iPhone) connections require brain-side approval. Only devices you approve can open Crypto Chat.
securityPage.faq5Q: What if policy requires air-gap or offline operation?
securityPage.faq5A: Connectivity is a choice. Leave company mail disconnected and keep thinking services off the public internet when policy requires it. The Sovereign still works as a local system under your roof.
securityPage.faq6Q: Do you train on our content or collect product telemetry?
securityPage.faq6A: Centurion Limited does not receive private Sovereign inference data to train shared models, and does not collect product telemetry or usage analytics from your private world. If you enable an external thinking service, only that provider call leaves your network under that provider’s terms.
securityPage.faq7Q: What compliance certifications do you claim?
securityPage.faq7A: We do not claim FedRAMP, SOC 2, IL ratings, or military authorisations. Our position is architectural: owner-controlled hardware, encrypted private chat on your network, optional offline operation, and IdP-governed connectors. For a private questionnaire, contact the security desk.
securityPage.faq8Q: How should we roll this out conservatively?
securityPage.faq8A: Start with the owner or CEO only on a dedicated host, manual approvals, no messaging gateway. Then add that owner’s Remote with pairing-only access. Optionally unlock Vault while still owner-only — see https://help.1human1ai.com/docs/privacy-and-security/memory-horizon-and-vault and https://help.1human1ai.com/docs/getting-started/onboarding-checklist. Expand users only after allowlists, provider review, and isolation choices are agreed with IT.
securityPage.controlApprovalsBody: Default manual approval for dangerous commands; configurable toolsets; MCP env filtering strips secrets unless explicitly allowed.
securityPage.controlGatewayBody: Default-deny messaging access. Empty allowlist means nobody. Pairing and allowlists gate who can talk to the agent.
securityPage.controlVaultBody: Hot Cache and Session Recall by default. Vault is opt-in encrypted long-term memory — deliberate propose/commit, not transcript dumps.
securityPage.ctaBody: Forward this page to your security team, download the one-page PDF, or ask us for a written response to your questionnaire.

securitySummary.title: Built so security teams can say yes.
securitySummary.lead: Private work stays on hardware you own. Crypto Chat stays on your network. Company mail connects only under your identity provider — never through a Centurion cloud.
securitySummary.bullets: Owned Sovereign — data sovereignty under your roof|Crypto Chat — phone to Sovereign, encrypted on your network|Offline capable when policy forbids public internet|Microsoft 365 & Google Workspace — OAuth, least privilege, IT revoke
securitySummary.cta: Read the Security brief for IT

counsel.title: Speak with Adrian
counsel.lead: I help people see whether a Sovereign belongs with them. Share what you are building toward.
counsel.expandedLead: Take your time. Your Sovereign lives with you — this conversation helps you see if one belongs beside you.
counsel.chips: I'm clarifying my life's mission|I'm on a board|Privacy is non-negotiable|Cloud AI isn't loyal enough
counsel.inputPlaceholder: What are you building your life toward?

features.lead: An AI you own completely. No company in the middle. No quiet copies of your life leaving home.
features.item1Description: Made to live in your home — crafted materials, beautiful to look at, ready for a desk you care about. Talk through your phone, or use the screen and keys on the Centurion itself.
features.item2Description: Talk to your Centurion through Crypto Chat: end-to-end encrypted on your network. Public AIs have no equivalent. When your world must stay sealed, Centurion can also run without any link to the public internet.
features.item3Title: Memory Horizon — remembers on your terms
features.item3Description: Hot Cache and Session Recall stay on your Sovereign by default. Optional Vault seals long-horizon notes under your passphrase — propose, then commit — without shipping that archive to a company cloud.
features.item4Description: Held in a heavy, impact-resistant case fit for a desk of consequence. Leather and alloys for a look that matches the work inside.

remote.lead: Talk to your Centurion on your desk through Crypto Chat — a private encrypted conversation on your home Wi‑Fi that only your Centurion and your phone can read. Public AIs have nothing like it. Sovereign Remote is your companion: Crypto Chat, guide your mission, watch work happen, and decide when you are needed — not another public chat box on the internet.
remote.imageAlt: Sovereign Remote on iPhone — Home view with mission work in progress
remote.capability1Description: Private encrypted conversation with your Centurion on your network — something public AIs simply do not offer.
remote.capability2Description: Open Soul Documents, briefings, and mission notes your Centurion keeps on your side of the wall.
remote.capability3Description: LAN-first Collaborations on your linked Sovereign — orient work, answer signals, and receive Ready for you outcomes. Invite other owners only when you choose.
remote.capability4Description: Hot Cache and Session Recall by default on the brain. Optional Vault is sealed long-term memory you unlock deliberately — Remote orients; the brain holds the store.
remote.capability5Description: See whether your days are lining up with the life you mean to live.
remote.capability6Description: Connection, how your AI thinks, look and feel, and personal rules — change them from your iPhone.
remote.diagramAria: Diagram of a Sovereign on a desk linked wirelessly to the Sovereign Remote on iPhone

metrics.metric1Label: Quiet leaks of your private life
metrics.metric2Label: You own your Centurion and your story
metrics.metric3Label: From first talk to placement in your home
metrics.metric4Label: Careful human support when you need it

howItWorks.lead: Your Sovereign comes to understand your world from what you choose to share with it — privately, on hardware you own. The company never receives that life. Place your Sovereign under your roof on your terms, then live with it every day: loyal, private, and quiet about what is yours alone.
howItWorks.diagramCaption: UNDER YOUR ROOF · ON YOUR TERMS
howItWorks.imageAlt: Sovereign on a private desk under a home roof — placed on your terms
howItWorks.step1Description: Your Sovereign comes to know how you live and work from what you choose to share with it — privacy, family, business, and your life’s mission. That understanding stays on your Sovereign. The company never receives it.
howItWorks.step2Description: Your Sovereign lives with you — at home or in your office. You choose how connected it is to the outside world, including fully offline when that matters.
howItWorks.step3Description: Your Sovereign works around the clock on your life’s mission, learns only from what you share with it, and never sells or shares your private world — including with the company that built it.
howItWorks.diagramAria: Diagram of Understand, Place, and Live — the three steps of bringing a Sovereign home

plainTalk.lead: Just talk plainly. Ask for what you want. Sovereign answers as it should — without making you learn a special language first.
plainTalk.publicBody: If you have to write a fancy prompt to get a useful answer, you are not “winning at AI.” You are fighting it — working around training that was shaped by big tech to seed ideas into the crowd and train you, instead of letting you train the AI on your life. That dance is the product for public AI.
plainTalk.sovereignBody: This one is different. Ask whatever you want, in ordinary words, and it gives you what you asked for. No tip sheets. No prompt engineering. No need to become an “expert prompter.” Just talk to it the way you would talk to someone who works for you — because that is what it is.

infrastructure.lead: Your Centurion is set up the way your life needs — in your house, in your office, fully offline, or a mix. You choose how much of the outside world it may ever touch.
infrastructure.locations: In your home|On your desk, under your roof|Yours||In your office|With the work that must stay private|Close||Fully offline|No link to the public internet|Sealed||Your choice of mix|As open or closed as life requires|Flexible

integrations.lead: By invitation and referral. For people who know that real privacy cannot be borrowed, rented, or shared.
integrations.audiences: Founders|Builders of empires||Investors|Guardians of capital||Collectors|Keepers of legacy||Families|Multi-generational wealth||Family Offices|Principals & advisors||Defense|Quiet, serious work||Statesmen|Discretion & trust||Institutions|Finance & governance

security.lead: Built for people who cannot put their life's work in a public chat box. Crypto Chat keeps your conversation with your Sovereign private and encrypted on your network — something public AIs do not have. Your Sovereign holds what you share. Sovereign the company does not — that private world never leaves your hardware. Privacy is not an add-on; it is the starting point.
security.trustSignals: Crypto Chat|Made for private lives|Nothing leaves without you|Built for serious work
security.imageAlt: Sealed private study — a Sovereign stays under your roof
security.feature1Description: End-to-end encrypted talk with your Sovereign on your network — a private channel public AIs do not offer at all.
security.feature2Description: When nothing may leave the room, Centurion can work without the public internet.
security.feature3Description: Access is careful and deliberate. Nothing is assumed safe without your say.
security.feature4Description: Hardware in your home. Control in your hands. Your private life does not leave with a vendor.
security.diagramAria: Diagram of your private world inside the home, sealed from public chat and cloud

developers.lead: Other AIs treat skills like apps from a shop — made by strangers. Centurion does the opposite. It does not trust skills made by others. Your Centurion builds what you need, when you need it, and improves as you go.
developers.riskTitle: Why skills from others are risky
developers.riskBody: A skill you did not write can quietly send information out, change how your AI behaves later, or hide instructions you never agreed to. That is not a shortcut. It is a door into your private life. Centurion keeps that door closed.
developers.imageAlt: Your Centurion building a skill at home — kept with you, not from a stranger store
developers.feature1Description: A skill written by someone else can hide quiet ways to leak your private life or change how your AI behaves. Centurion will not take that risk for you.
developers.feature2Description: It is not scrolling a store of other people's tools. It builds what you need from your mission and your way of working.
developers.feature3Description: When work opens a gap, Centurion creates the skill it needs — then keeps it with you, not on someone else's shelf.
developers.feature4Description: Notice what happened. Act. Learn. Improve. Skills tighten as you live with them — without sending your life back to a vendor.
developers.closingTitle: Made for you, kept with you
developers.closingBody: Skills your Centurion makes stay in your home. They serve your life's mission — not someone else's product plan.
developers.diagramAria: Diagram showing stranger skill stores blocked while your Centurion makes skills at home

testimonials.trustedBy: Trusted by those who value discretion
testimonials.testimonial1Quote: My life's work, my family's legacy, my private letters — I refuse to let any of it sit in a public chat. Centurion understands what privacy truly means.
testimonials.testimonial2Quote: Public AI answers questions. Centurion knows you. It is a private study, built inside your home, furnished exactly to your mind.
testimonials.testimonial3Quote: For those who cannot afford to trust third parties with their intelligence. It answers only to me, and it always will.

limitedEdition.successAlert: Thank you — your Centurion range interest is registered.

cta.lead: For people who have built remarkable lives and need an AI worthy of that care. Buy once — free lifetime updates, no monthly fees — and learn with other owners every Sunday night.

privacy.metaDescription: How Sovereign collects and protects personal data.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: {company} (“we”) builds private AI systems that run on customer-owned hardware. This policy explains how we handle personal data when you use our website, ordering system, and support services.
privacy.collectBody: We collect information you provide when registering interest, placing a quote, or contacting support — including name, email, phone, company, and order configuration. We also collect technical logs necessary to operate our services.
privacy.useBody: We use your data to process enquiries and orders, deliver and support Centurion systems, communicate about your account, and improve our services. We do not use your private Centurion inference data to train shared models.
privacy.rightsBody: Under UK GDPR you may request access, correction, deletion, or portability of your personal data. Contact us at {email}.

terms.metaDescription: Terms governing use of Sovereign website, ordering, and related services.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of {company}'s website, ordering platform, and related services. By submitting an enquiry or creating an account, you agree to these terms.
terms.hardwareBody: Centurion systems combine dedicated hardware with proprietary AI software configured for a single principal. Ownership of hardware transfers on delivery; software is licensed for use on that hardware subject to your order and support arrangements.
terms.paymentBody: Quotes and deposits are denominated in GBP unless otherwise agreed. Website quotes do not charge cards automatically — deposits and balances are arranged privately with our team. Deposits secure production slots.
terms.dataBody: Your Sovereign runs on infrastructure you control. We do not access your private inference data except as required for agreed support, security updates, or legal obligation.

familyBeneficiary.metaDescription: Introduce four colleagues who buy Centurion — earn one complimentary Centurion for someone you love.
familyBeneficiary.title: Family Beneficiary Program
familyBeneficiary.lead: Introduce four colleagues who buy Centurion — earn one for someone you love.
familyBeneficiary.remoteImageAlt: Sovereign Remote on iPhone — Family Beneficiary Program on the You tab
familyBeneficiary.sceneImageAlt: Two Sovereigns on a private desk — one yours, one earned for someone you love
familyBeneficiary.steps: Nominate a family beneficiary — first name only. Often a spouse or family member who wants a Sovereign after seeing yours thrive.|Refer colleagues — people who will buy their own Sovereign. Family members are who you are earning for; they cannot be counted as referrals.|Four successful purchases — when four referred colleagues complete their buy, you earn one complimentary Centurion for your nominated family beneficiary.|Start again — cycles reset without limit. Nominate a new family beneficiary and keep going.
familyBeneficiary.summaryItems: 4 colleague purchases = 1 family Centurion|Referrals: colleagues only (non-family)|Reward: complimentary Centurion for your nominated family member|Manage it in Sovereign Remote under You → Family Beneficiary Program
familyBeneficiary.manageBody: Open Sovereign Remote on your iPhone, go to the You tab, and use the Family Beneficiary Program card. There you can nominate (or change) your beneficiary, refer colleagues, and track pending versus purchased progress toward your family Centurion.
familyBeneficiary.confirmation: A referral counts when Centurion operations confirms the colleague's purchase — not the moment you add their name in the app.
familyBeneficiary.rules: Family members are beneficiaries, not referral targets — listing family as a colleague referral is not allowed.|You must attest that each referred colleague is not a family member when you add them.|The complimentary unit is for the family beneficiary you nominated for that cycle.|Program details may be updated; the authoritative progress always shows in your Remote app when linked to your Sovereign.
familyBeneficiary.contactBody: Questions about a referral or a family Centurion you have earned? Write to {email} or use Ask Adrian in the help center.

whitepaper.metaDescription: A private AI for a private life. Ownership under your roof, loyalty by design, and a sealed study for your life's mission.
whitepaper.title: Sovereign: A private AI for a private life
whitepaper.lead: One human. One AI. Computation under your roof — loyal because you own it, sealed because your private world was never meant for a shared queue.
whitepaper.abstractP1: Public AI systems are built for scale: many people, one platform, one pool of attention. Sovereign is built for the opposite shape — a single principal, a machine that lives with them, and a relationship that deepens without leaking into someone else’s product.
whitepaper.abstractP2: This paper describes the idea in plain terms: what Centurion is, how the system is shaped, where trust stops, and why a private study — not a rented chatbot — is the right form for serious private work.
whitepaper.fig1: Figure 1 · Public attention versus a sealed home study
whitepaper.problemP1: Much of what people need from AI is intimate: family matters, business judgments, letters that should never travel, and the slow work of naming a life’s mission. Shared platforms excel at answers. They are poorly suited to stewardship of a private world.
whitepaper.problemP2: When your context lives on someone else’s servers, loyalty is a policy — and policies change. Privacy becomes a promise about what a vendor will do, not a fact about where the machine sits. Principals who have built empires and guard a legacy need something quieter and harder: a sealed study under their own roof.
whitepaper.whatP1: A Sovereign is an owned appliance — a private brain on your network — paired with Sovereign Remote on iPhone. You talk to your Centurion, not to a crowd’s chatbot. Hardware stays with you. Control stays with you. The company that builds Centurion does not receive your private life as fuel.
whitepaper.whatP2: Remote is the companion in your pocket: counsel, mission, and the day’s work — always against the desk machine that holds Memory Horizon (Hot Cache, Session Recall, and optional Vault), skills, and history. Conversations travel as Crypto Chat: a private encrypted link on your network that public AIs do not offer. The link is yours. The store is yours.
whitepaper.fig2: Figure 2 · System shape: device link inside the home; thinking services optional and outside the private store
whitepaper.shapeLead: Three ideas stay separate on purpose.
whitepaper.shapeItems: Device link|your iPhone talks to your Sovereign over your network. That path is the relationship.||Private store|conversations, mission, skills, and decision history live on the brain you own.||Thinking service|when you choose an external model to help with reasoning, keys stay on the brain. The optional hop is not the same thing as surrendering your archive.
whitepaper.shapeClose: You may run fully offline when the room must stay sealed. Connectivity is a dial you set — not a default that assumes the public internet.
whitepaper.fig3: Figure 3 · Understand, Place, Live — a continuous loop
whitepaper.livesLead: Centurion is not installed once and forgotten. It enters a life in three movements that repeat:
whitepaper.livesSteps: Understand|it comes to know how you live and work from what you choose to share: privacy, family, business, and mission. That understanding stays on the machine.||Place|it settles at home or in the office on your terms, including fully offline when that matters.||Live|it works with you every day, learns only from what you give it, and does not sell or share your private world — including with the company that built it.
whitepaper.fig4: Figure 4 · Trust boundary — what stays home never crosses
whitepaper.privacyP1: Sovereignty here is physical and operational: hardware in your space, deliberate access, a clear history of decisions that remains with you. Nothing is assumed safe without your say.
whitepaper.privacyP2: The wall is the point. Conversations, mission documents, forged skills, and the trail of what your Sovereign did — those stay on your side. The company and public chat sit outside the ellipse. When you need silence, Centurion can work without the public internet.
whitepaper.fig5: Figure 5 · Stranger skill stores refused; skills forged under your roof
whitepaper.skillsP1: Other systems treat skills like apps from a shop — made by strangers, downloaded into trusted places. Centurion refuses that door. A skill from someone you do not know can hide quiet ways to leak a private life or change how an AI behaves.
whitepaper.skillsP2: When work opens a gap, your Sovereign forges the skill it needs and keeps it with you. Skills serve your mission. They do not arrive from a shelf designed for everyone.
whitepaper.missionP1: A life’s mission is the organizing purpose — not a feature checkbox. Sovereign Remote is the daily surface: talk, clarify, watch progress, return to the work that matters. The desk Sovereign holds the depth; the phone carries the thread.
whitepaper.missionP2: Understanding a mission is not the same as writing a soul document, and neither is meant for a shared platform’s retention policy. Both stay in the sealed study.
whitepaper.closingP1: Sovereign is a bet on form: owned hardware, a pocket companion, skills made at home, and a trust boundary that does not depend on a vendor’s mood. Real privacy cannot be borrowed, rented, or shared.
whitepaper.closingP2: If that shape belongs with you, secure a build slot — or read how people set up and live with Centurion in the support library.
whitepaper.diagramThesisAria: Diagram contrasting a public cloud attention marketplace with a sealed home study holding a Centurion
whitepaper.diagramSystemAria: Diagram of person, iPhone Remote, home network, Centurion brain, and optional thinking services outside the private store
whitepaper.diagramLifeAria: Diagram of Understand, Place, and Live as a continuous lifecycle loop
whitepaper.diagramTrustAria: Diagram of the trust boundary: private data stays home; company and public chat stay outside
whitepaper.diagramSkillsAria: Diagram showing stranger skill stores locked out while Centurion forges skills at home
